locations.name: 约翰·古尔德
locations.summary: 约翰·古尔德 FRS（英語：John Gould，1804年9月14日—1881年2月3日）是19世纪一位英国鸟类学家。1804年生于英格兰，也是一位商人、艺术家。1838年后与其妻一同前往澳大利亚，在当地发现并為许多新物种命名。 早年…

locations.name: 澳大利亚
locations.summary: 澳大利亚联邦（英語：Commonwealth of Australia），通称澳大利亚、澳洲（Australia，缩写为AU、AUS），是一个位于南半球中北部、東半球东部的議會制君主立憲制、聯邦制國家。全国整体属于大洋洲（並非自成一洲），…

locations.name: 印度尼西亚
locations.summary: 印度尼西亞共和国（印尼語：Republik Indonesia，IPA讀音：[rɛpʊblɪk ɪndɔnɛsɪa]），通稱印度尼西亞，簡稱印尼，是位于东南亚的群岛国家；由17,506個岛屿组成，是世界上最大的群島國家，疆域橫跨亞洲及大洋…

locations.name: 巴布亚新几内亚

locations.name: 北領地
locations.summary: 北領地（英語：Northern Territory，縮寫為NT），別稱北方领地地区、北部地方、北澳，是澳大利亞的一個聯邦領地，位於澳洲大陸的中北部，北臨帝汶海、阿拉弗拉海與卡奔塔利亞灣，西接西澳州，南鄰南澳州，東界昆士蘭州。其總面積廣達1…

locations.name: 金伯利 (西澳)
locations.summary: 金伯利（Kimberley）是澳大利亚西澳大利亚州的九个地区之一，位于西澳州的北部，西临印度洋，北临帝汶海，南为大沙沙漠和塔纳米沙漠，东则与澳大利亚北领地接壤。 该地区以位于南非的金伯利钻石矿区命名，因这两个区域在地形、风景和资源方面均有…

locations.name: 梅尔维尔岛 (澳大利亚)
locations.summary: 梅尔维尔岛（Melville Island），在当地提维语中的名称是耶尔马尔纳（Yermalner），位于帝汶海东部，在澳大利亚北领地外海，與澳大利亞本土之間相隔著范迪門海灣（Van Diemen Gulf）；达尔文的北侧；阿纳姆地（Ar…

locations.name: Darwin Harbour
locations.summary: Darwin Harbour is a body of water close to the city of Darwin in the Northern Territory of Australia.

locations.name: 三角洲
locations.summary: 三角洲是指河流於出海口形成的沖積平原，是一种常见的地表形貌。江河奔流中所裹挟的泥沙等杂质，在入海口处遇到含盐量较淡水高得多的海水，凝絮淤积，逐渐成为河口岸边新的湿地，继而形成三角洲平原。三角洲的顶部指向河流上游，外缘面向大海，可以看作是三…

relations.subject: Red-headed myzomela
relations.evidence: The red-headed myzomela or red-headed honeyeater (Myzomela erythrocephala) is a passerine bird of the honeyeater family Meliphagidae found in Australia, Indonesia, and Papua New Guinea

relations.subject: Red-headed myzomela
relations.evidence: thumb|right|A juvenile The nominate subspecies of the red-headed myzomela is distributed across the tropical coastlines of Western Australia, the Northern Territory and Queensland. It inhabits coastal areas of the Kimberley and various offshore islands in Western Australia, and is similarly distributed in the Northern Territory, including Melville Island and the Sir Edward Pellew Group of Islands

relations.subject: Red-headed myzomela
relations.evidence: The peak abundance of the species in the mangroves around Darwin Harbour during the mid-dry and early wet season coincided with the production of young and the flowering of the yellow mangrove (Ceriops australis). The species' movements are poorly understood, variously described as resident, nomadic or migratory. Population numbers have been reported as fluctuating in some areas with local movements possibly related to the flowering of preferred mangrove and Melaleuca food trees, and there is some indication that the birds can travel more widely. A single bird was recaptured after being banded nearly five years earlier, from the original banding site, and the species' occupation of a large number of offshore islands suggests that the red-headed myzomela is effective at crossing distances over water. The maximum age recorded from banding has been 7years 1.5months. The red-headed myzomela mostly inhabits mangroves in monsoonal coastal areas, especially thickets of spotted mangrove (Rhizophora stylosa), smallflower bruguiera (Bruguiera parviflora) and grey mangrove (Avicennia marina) bordering islands or in river deltas, but it often also occurs in paperbark thickets fringing the mangroves such as those of cajeput (Melaleuca leucadendra). It is a mangrove specialist, an adaptation that probably occurred as northern Australia became more arid and the bird populations became dependent on mangroves as other types of forest disappeared. The mangroves provide nectar and insects as well as shelter and nesting sites, and they supply the majority of the species' needs for most of the year. In Australia, mangrove vegetation forms a narrow discontinuous strip along thousands of kilometres of coastline, accommodating birds specialized for the habitat. Eighty Mile Beach in Western Australia has no mangroves and no fringing Melaleuca forests, reducing its potential for successful colonization by nectarivores, and it marks the southern limit of the red-headed myzomela in Western Australia. The red-headed myzomela is occasionally found in swampy woodlands, casuarina woodlands and open forest, particularly those with Pandanus and paperbarks, as well as on coconut farms
